programme.title: NITV on the Road: Yabun
programme.desc: From our travelling music series, we showcase veterans and newcomers alike as they perform up on the Yabun stage at Victoria Park, Sydney.

programme.title: Nitv On The Road: Barunga Festival
programme.desc: From our travelling music series, we showcase veterans and newcomers alike as they perform at the Barunga Festival 2015.

programme.title: NITV on the Road: Boomerang Festival
programme.desc: Boomerang is a festival held in Byron Bay over the long weekend. It is run by Rhoda Roberts, the creator of the Dreaming Festival, and is a mixture of Australian and international Indigenous acts.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.desc: The best coastlines are in Western Australia and Tasmania. Don't believe us? Check out this series as it returns for its 6th season, highlighting some of Australia's breathtaking landscapes.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.desc: We journey through the famed Margaret River region, home to rolling vineyards, towering Karri forests, and powerful surf breaks. This episode explores the land's natural contrasts and the deep Noongar stories held within it.

programme.title: Toi Time
programme.desc: Maia, Tama and Jojo make a train in the Mahi Toi room while they wait for the rain to stop outside and Maia and Wolfy look for Oma in the Paw Paw patch.

programme.title: Waabiny Time
programme.desc: The team explore some of the words the Noongar people use for the ocean, and the animals that live in the sea.

programme.title: Bird's Eye View
programme.desc: The humans love to clean, so why not help them have a cleaning party! The best way to help out? By dive bombing them with some well placed, certified organic tiko. Splat!

programme.title: Aussie Bush Tales
programme.desc: Snowy the dingo puppy is very restless when he hears other dingoes howling in the night. He runs away to investigate.

programme.title: The Mysterious Cities of Gold
programme.desc: In the middle of the cave, at the top of the pyramid, our friends find an orichalcum sphere, floating on a lake. They get attacked by thousands of giant crabs but manage to seek refuge inside.

programme.title: Bushwhacked!
programme.desc: Kayne and Kamil are on a soaring mission from Perth to Lorna Glen deep in the Western Australia desert, where Kayne must follow and observe the movements of a wedge-tailed eagle.

programme.title: Motown Magic
programme.sub-title: You are the Sunshine of My Life/I'll Be Doggone
programme.desc: Imaginative boy Ben transforms his city by bringing colourful street art to life, armed with a magic paintbrush and the classic sounds of motown.

programme.title: The Magic Canoe
programme.desc: Pam doesn't say what she really wants and accumulates frustrations. When she meets the chicoque (skunk in the Cree and Metis language), she realises that it would be better to say what bothers her.

programme.title: Spartakus and the Sun Beneath the Sea
programme.sub-title: The Emperor Qing and the Eighth Kingdom
programme.desc: Having conquered seven kingdoms and built a gigantic wall, the tyrannical emperor Qing decides to conquer the eighth kingdom, the city of Arkadia, with his army of clay soldiers.

programme.title: The World According to Grandpa
programme.desc: Stanley asks 'What are clouds for?' and Grandpa spins one of his tall tales about his own Grandpa who used to fly his own cloud all over the world, rescuing naughty animals.

programme.title: Chums
programme.desc: A baby skunk has a hard time making friends because of her stinky reputation. The Chums set things right with the help of Moise and Maurice the Mooses.

programme.title: Mysteries of the Mekong
programme.desc: Squeezed between South East Asia's two most formidable geographical barriers - the Annamite mountains and the Mekong river, lies Khammouane.

programme.title: NITV News Update
programme.desc: NITV News recaptures the highlights of Indigenous news around the country.

programme.title: Buffalo Legends
programme.desc: The story of a group of men who knocked down the barriers of racism on the sporting field in Darwin.

programme.title: Pro Bull Riding - USA
programme.desc: High-stakes bull riding. Rivalries ignite, champions rise, and every ride pushes the limits in a thrilling new season of action, danger, and determination. Tune in for pure adrenaline.

programme.title: Over the Black Dot
programme.desc: In 2026, Over the Black Dot becomes a digital-first Rugby League yarn free-flowing conversations on footy, culture and community, with panellists and guests sharing authentic Indigenous stories.

programme.title: Inside the Huddle
programme.desc: What is it like to be a footballer? Candid conversations, laughs, and insights that go beyond the game and into the outer.

programme.title: On Country Kitchen
programme.desc: Mark and Derek begin their journey with a Welcome to Yuin Country from Noel Butler, harvest oysters off the bay, stop through at an olive grove, and try stand-up paddleboarding on the NSW coastline.

programme.title: The Cook Up with Adam Liaw
programme.desc: Adam is joined by cult film star Pia Miranda, and 'beersotto' inventor Warren Mendes to show you how sometimes, a quick dish is the best dish.

programme.title: Spartakus and the Sun Beneath the Sea
programme.desc: Tired from travelling, Tehrig must stop in a snowy steppe. There our heroes meet Myra and her grandfather, whose village is regularly looted by warrior Mogokhs.

programme.title: The Mysterious Cities of Gold
programme.desc: The original 1980s animation classic that follows a young orphan called Esteban, as he searches the new world for both his father and the mysterious Cities of Gold.

programme.title: The Mysterious Cities of Gold
programme.desc: The original 1980s animation classic that follows a young orphan called Esteban, as he searches the new world for both his father and the mysterious Cities of Gold.

programme.title: Little J & Big Cuz
programme.desc: Big Cuz tricks Little J into believing that the Giant Wombat is not extinct.

programme.title: Motown Magic
programme.sub-title: You are the Sunshine of My Life/I'll Be Doggone
programme.desc: Imaginative boy Ben transforms his city by bringing colourful street art to life, armed with a magic paintbrush and the classic sounds of motown.

programme.title: Harlem Globetrotters: Play It Forward
programme.desc: Hotshot, Cheese, and Wham learn about an organization that builds playgrounds for kids of all abilities.

programme.title: Garma: Celebrating 25 Years
programme.desc: Stories from Yolngu Country celebrating 25 years of Garma.

programme.title: Te Ao with Moana
programme.desc: A weekly current affairs show that examines New Zealand and international stories through a Maori lens.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.desc: Slow TV is back, celebrating stunning landscapes of Countries across Australia. Sit back and relax with the healing powers of Country.

programme.title: NITV News Update
programme.desc: NITV News recaptures the highlights of Indigenous news around the country.

programme.title: Mysteries of the Mekong
programme.desc: As the Mekong carves its way down Thailand's eastern border, huge expanses of jungle are broken up by a network of waterways.

programme.title: NITV Weather
programme.desc: Australian First Nations essential weekend weather forecasts, preparedness tips and cultural knowledge.

programme.title: Going Places With Ernie Dingo
programme.desc: Ernie returns to the Great Barrier Reef to meet the young mob helping to restore it and goes croc-spotting on the Daintree River. Brooke walks the coast to catch a feed of mud crab.

programme.title: True Colours
programme.desc: News of Mariah's death has reached the community. Mariah's family gather on the outskirts of town for Sorry Business. With Nick by her side, Toni observes the ceremony from a distance. She wants to talk with Mariah's family but knows she has to respect the cultural mourning.

programme.title: Mad Max
programme.desc: In a lawless future, a brutal biker gang pushes one cop too far. After losing everything, Max Rockatansky becomes a one-man vengeance machine in this raw, relentless tale of justice and revenge.

programme.title: My Survival as an Aboriginal
programme.desc: Essie Coffey, a black activist and musician, shows the conflicts of living as an Aboriginal under white domination.

programme.title: Patou: In Black and White
programme.desc: With a career that spans over four decades and across the globe, Jamaican singer Pat Powell is one of Australia's most accomplished yet hidden vocal talents.

programme.title: The Redfern Story
programme.desc: A small group of passionate visionaries pioneered Redfern's National Black Theatre in the 1970's, a politically volatile time in our history, transforming Australian culture and politics forever.

programme.title: NITV on the Road: Yabun
programme.desc: From our travelling music series, we showcase veterans and newcomers alike as they perform up on the Yabun stage at Victoria Park, Sydney.

programme.title: Nitv On The Road: Barunga Festival
programme.desc: From our travelling music series, we showcase veterans and newcomers alike as they perform at the Barunga Festival 2015.

programme.title: NITV on the Road: Boomerang Festival
programme.desc: Boomerang is a festival held in Byron Bay over the long weekend. It is run by Rhoda Roberts, the creator of the Dreaming Festival, and is a mixture of Australian and international Indigenous acts.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.desc: Following the final stretch of the Cape-to-Cape track, we arrive at Cape Leeuwin, where two oceans meet. This episode reveals the spiritual and geographic significance of this place to the Wadandi-Pibulmun people, framed by striking coastal views and limestone cliffs.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.desc: In the first of this four-part journey, we head to Parperloihener, Pennemuker, Peerapper, Manegin and Tarkiner Country. Here, wild seas crash into ancient shorelines at places like Arthur River, the Edge of the World and windswept Kennaook, Cape Grim, while exploring the deep spiritual and ecological significance of this dramatic landscape.

programme.title: Toi Time
programme.desc: The Toi Time whanau find out what letters are and how different languages use different letters. Aunty Anika shows us how important it is to tidy our room.

programme.title: Waabiny Time
programme.desc: The team shares ways to stay healthy.

programme.title: Bird's Eye View
programme.desc: Golf. Rugby. Soccer. Humans really give those eggs a hard time! After one of the 'eggs' makes its way into the tree, the birds take care of it like their own pepe.

programme.title: Aussie Bush Tales
programme.desc: Moort sees eyes shining in the moonlight and is convinced that a tiger is hiding in the bush, waiting to get him.

programme.title: The Mysterious Cities of Gold
programme.desc: Inside the city's treasure room, our friends discover that they're able to control the orichalcum cubes with their thoughts, thanks to the crowns they're wearing.

programme.title: Bushwhacked!
programme.desc: The beautiful Noosa coastline is the backdrop for a shower that Kayne won't be forgetting in a hurry.

programme.title: Motown Magic
programme.sub-title: He's Misstra Know It All/You Can't Hurry Love
programme.desc: Imaginative boy Ben transforms his city by bringing colourful street art to life, armed with a magic paintbrush and the classic sounds of motown.

programme.title: The Magic Canoe
programme.desc: Nico makes others angry because he 'cries wolf' to get their attention. His comical adventure will make him realise that 'crying wolf' can have unpleasant consequences.

programme.title: Spartakus and the Sun Beneath the Sea
programme.desc: On Arkadia, the Tehrig's disease has worsened. Not hearing any messages coming back, the Arkadians decide to try themselves to save their sun.

programme.title: The World According to Grandpa
programme.desc: Connie asks 'Why is chocolate so tasty?' and Grandpa tells of a time when chocolate wasn't tasty, and when its inventor, Lambeth O'Gravy, flew into space looking for magical ingredients!

programme.title: Chums
programme.desc: Pterry must help Moise the moose and Maurice the moose who get lost while looking for a new grazing pasture of wild rice.

programme.title: Mysteries of the Mekong
programme.desc: As the Mekong carves its way down Thailand's eastern border, huge expanses of jungle are broken up by a network of waterways.

programme.title: NITV News Update
programme.desc: NITV News recaptures the highlights of Indigenous news around the country.

programme.title: Going Places With Ernie Dingo
programme.desc: Ernie returns to the Great Barrier Reef to meet the young mob helping to restore it and goes croc-spotting on the Daintree River. Brooke walks the coast to catch a feed of mud crab.

programme.title: Alick and Albert
programme.desc: Even though they live worlds apart, Torres Strait artist and activist Alick Tipoti and His Serene Highness Prince Albert II of Monaco unite to help protect the ocean.

programme.title: Turn Up Respect
programme.desc: Featuring the stories of people and prevention programs that are teaching respect in community.

programme.title: On Country Kitchen
programme.desc: On the coast at Jervis Bay, Derek helps Joel and Adrian to dive for lobsters off the rocks while Mark tends to the fire; in Ulladulla, the boys get a taste for the cheesemaking life with Rosie and Angel, and relax on the picturesque deck with a wine and cheese in hand, soaking up the mid-afternoon sun; inland at Milton, the boys are put to the task of eating as little produce as possible when chocolate-making with Jo and Dan; Mark and Derek indulge in a heart-to-heart kayaking down the Kangaroo Valley River.

programme.title: The Cook Up with Adam Liaw
programme.desc: Adam, chef Hamed Allahyari and writer Dani Valent channel all their energy into decadent desserts.

programme.title: Spartakus and the Sun Beneath the Sea
programme.desc: Spartakus and three pirates have been taken prisoner by a Sultana, who reigns over a city where women enslave men. Arkana and Massmedia are forced to form an alliance to try to free their friends.

programme.title: The Mysterious Cities of Gold
programme.desc: The original 1980s animation classic that follows a young orphan called Esteban, as he searches the new world for both his father and the mysterious Cities of Gold.

programme.title: The Mysterious Cities of Gold
programme.desc: The original 1980s animation classic that follows a young orphan called Esteban, as he searches the new world for both his father and the mysterious Cities of Gold.

programme.title: Little J & Big Cuz
programme.desc: When the 'big kids' won't play with him, Little J creates a tantalising adventure - in the backyard.

programme.title: Motown Magic
programme.sub-title: He's Misstra Know It All/You Can't Hurry Love
programme.desc: Imaginative boy Ben transforms his city by bringing colourful street art to life, armed with a magic paintbrush and the classic sounds of motown.

programme.title: Harlem Globetrotters: Play It Forward
programme.desc: Bulldog, Hotshot, Hammer, and Coach Lou challenge Little Brothers and Little Sisters to an unforgettable game of basketball.

programme.title: Garma: Celebrating 25 Years
programme.desc: Stories from Yolngu Country celebrating 25 years of Garma.

programme.title: NITV News: Nula
programme.desc: The latest news from the oldest living culture, join Natalie Ahmat and the team of journalists for stories from an Indigenous perspective.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.desc: Slow TV is back, celebrating stunning landscapes of Countries across Australia. Sit back and relax with the healing powers of Country.

programme.title: Where the Dreamings Come From
programme.desc: This film documents the visit of the Areyonga School back to Uluru and Mutitjulu, where elders pass onto the younger generation their Tjukurpa and share the dreamings of their ancestors.

programme.title: Mysteries of the Mekong
programme.desc: Although the Mekong runs down Thailand's far eastern border, the influence of the river's basin reaches across nearly half the country.

programme.title: NITV Weather
programme.desc: Australian First Nations essential weekend weather forecasts, preparedness tips and cultural knowledge.

programme.title: Castle In The Sky
programme.desc: Sheeta, an orphan and a descendant of the people who once lived in Laputa, is taken by the secret service, led by agent Muska, who wants to find the legendary floating city of Laputa. Her only connection with the past is a stone, inscribed with a strange emblem. Pirates attack the secret service's airship, and in the commotion, Sheeta falls. As she does, the stone lights up and slows her descent. She floats down into the arms of Pazu, an orphan boy who works in the mines below. The two together must face Muska, and Sheeta must look into her past to protect her family's legacy. Directed by Hayao Miyazaki and stars the voices of Anna Paquin and James Van Der Beek.

programme.title: First Nation Bedtime Stories
programme.sub-title: The Cockatoo Sisters and the Magic Digging Stick
programme.desc: The story of two very different sisters, Walibun and Yaranari, who have the responsibility of caring for their grandfather, Yoongoorrookoo, the serpent man.

programme.title: Hey, Viktor!
programme.desc: Twenty-five long years after his time in the limelight, former child actor Cody Lightning tries to revive his fortunes with a self-produced sequel to Smoke Signals.

programme.title: NITV News: Nula
programme.desc: The latest news from the oldest living culture, join Natalie Ahmat and the team of journalists for stories from an Indigenous perspective.

programme.title: Sam Watson: The Street Fighting Years
programme.desc: An exclusive insight into the life and times of Aboriginal revolutionary Sam Watson during the violent days of Queensland's Bjelke-Petersen government.

programme.title: Black Man's Houses
programme.desc: This documentary tells the story of black survival in Tasmania amidst the continuing suppression of history and culture.

programme.title: NITV on the Road: Yabun
programme.desc: From our travelling music series, we showcase veterans and newcomers alike as they perform up on the Yabun stage at Victoria Park, Sydney.

programme.title: Nitv On The Road: Barunga Festival
programme.desc: From our travelling music series, we showcase veterans and newcomers alike as they perform at the Barunga Festival 2015.

programme.title: NITV on the Road: Boomerang Festival
programme.desc: Boomerang is a festival held in Byron Bay over the long weekend. It is run by Rhoda Roberts, the creator of the Dreaming Festival, and is a mixture of Australian and international Indigenous acts.
